I'll develop a code snippet that implements bubble sort to sort an array of strings in reverse alphabetical order. The code will not use built-in sorting methods and will handle duplicates properly.

```python
def reverse_alphabetical_sort(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(0, n-i-1):
            # Compare current elements in reverse alphabetical order
            if arr[j] < arr[j+1]:
                # Swap elements if they are in the wrong order
                arr[j], arr[j+1] = arr[j+1], arr[j]
    return arr

# Example usage
input_array = ['pear', 'peach', 'plum']
sorted_array = reverse_alphabetical_sort(input_array)
print(sorted_array)
```

This implementation uses bubble sort to sort the array in reverse alphabetical order. The `reverse_alphabetical_sort` function compares elements in the array and swaps them if they are out of order, ensuring the array is sorted correctly.
